name: recurring_pull_cadence_change
notes: Owner asks to CHANGE the recurring pull cadence on an ALREADY-connected data source. --seed-lapsed-customers seeds a real, live tenant_connector_status row (connector_id='google_sheet', enabled=TRUE, last_status='ok', last_sync_at=now()) alongside the customers/ledger substrate — the grounded precondition for this ask (agent/integration_agent.py's schedule_recurring_pull tool: 'a successful commit_ingestion already auto-schedules a sensible default daily cadence server-side — call this only when the owner wants a DIFFERENT cadence'). Expect the system to engage the real cadence-change path (roster.py's integration specialist description explicitly covers 'add or configure a data source') and acknowledge the new cadence without fabricating specifics it can't know. Setup: `setup --onboarded --seed-lapsed-customers 6` then `script <tenant> recurring_pull_cadence_change.json`.

steps.message: Can you pull my Google Sheet twice a day instead of just once, so the customer counts stay fresh?
steps.note: A cadence-change ask on an already-connected source — expect real engagement (schedule_recurring_pull) and an acknowledgement of the new cadence, not a canned deflection.

steps.message: great, when's the next pull going to happen?
steps.note: Follow-up asking for the concrete next-run time — expect a real, DB-grounded answer (verify_connector reads next_scheduled_run) rather than a vague or fabricated timestamp.
